Fix git-status URL race: don't overwrite URL on session change

onSessionChange was calling updateAppletUrlParam which triggered
history.replaceState, firing a Navigation API 'replace' event that
overwrote the user's explicitly navigated path. Now onSessionChange
only updates internal state and refreshes — URL stays as navigated.

fix: backtesting audit — slippage direction, time-exit slippage, ProfitFactor basis

AUDIT FIX 2026-07: Three bugs found and fixed:

1. TradeSimulator.ApplySlippage: Used isFavorable (tpHit) as direction
   toggle, which was wrong for 2/4 scenarios (short TP and long SL got
   favorable slippage instead of unfavorable). Now uses isLong for
   correct direction: long close = sell = price DOWN, short close = buy
   = price UP.

2. TradeSimulator quick-sim + full SimulateTrade: Time-based exits and
   end-of-data exits did not apply slippage at all, despite being market
   orders. Now consistently applies slippage to all exit types.

3. CausalBacktestingService + AnalyticsCausalBacktestingService:
   GrossProfit/GrossLoss tracked raw pips instead of USD (realizedPnL).
   This made ProfitFactor misleading across mixed asset types.
   BacktestingService was already fixed (AUDIT FIX 2026-06); now all
   services are consistent.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix cgroup check to be fully idempotent

Previous version used || which would re-run sed if either flag was
missing individually, potentially doubling flags and breaking boot.

New logic:
- Uses && so it only skips if BOTH flags are already present
- Strips any partial/duplicate flags before writing
- Prints the resulting cmdline.txt so you can verify before rebooting

Safe to run multiple times on the same node.

kernel: fix WiFi by adding mask_data0 to sun9i_a80_cfg

sun9i_a80_cfg in drivers/mmc/host/sunxi-mmc.c was missing mask_data0=true,
causing every MMC clock update on mmc1 (BCM4330 SDIO) to time out with
WAIT_PRE_OVER while DATA0 was held low by the uninitialised chip. Each
timeout set priv->fatal_err=1, permanently preventing card initialisation.

Setting mask_data0=true makes the driver set SDXC_MASK_DATA0 (CLKCR bit 31)
before clock-only updates, so the hardware ignores DATA0 state and updates
complete immediately. BCM4330 then initialises normally and wlan0 appears.

All other recent SoC configs (sun50i_a64, sun20i_d1, sun50i_h616) already
had this flag set; sun9i was the only one missing it.

- patches/kernel/0003-mmc-sunxi-Add-mask_data0-to-sun9i-A80-config.patch

feat: UX overhaul — lifecycle redesign, labels, structured status, CLI improvements (#2)

Comprehensive UX overhaul based on multi-model CLI audit and review.

Breaking changes (v0.1 → v0.2):
- pause/resume removed — use restart or stop+start instead
- run requires -- separator to prevent flag typos becoming commands
- run no longer accepts positional name — use --name/-n exclusively
- run with same name auto-replaces (no confirmation prompt)
- JSON schema changed — ls/status emit structured status with typed
  sub-states (RunningInfo, ExitedInfo, DeadInfo)
- ls default columns: NAME, STATUS, PORTS, AGE, COMMAND
  Use --wide for ID, PID, labels
- logs defaults to current run — use --all/-a for full history
- Confirmation prompts removed (kubectl-style: command = intent)
- Labels are validated: must start with letter, [a-zA-Z0-9._:-], 1-63 chars

Lifecycle redesign:
- restart: SIGHUP to supervisor, respawn child (same env, no backoff)
- start: re-launch stopped/exited task (new supervisor process)

New commands:
- label <name> [LABEL...] — set/replace/clear labels on existing tasks
- cleanup — remove state for all non-running tasks

New flags:
- run -e KEY=VAL, -d DIR — short flags restored
- stop --all, stop --timeout, stop/rm/restart/start --labels (repeatable)
- rm --force/-f, rm --all, rm --labels
- ls --wide, ls --no-trunc; logs --all/-a
- Variadic names: bgtask stop foo bar baz
- --restart on-failure — restart on non-zero exit (vs always = any exit)

Structured task status:
- TaskStatus type with RunningInfo/ExitedInfo/DeadInfo sub-states
- STATUS column includes uptime/since (e.g. running (5m), exited(0) (2m ago))
- AGE column shows time since task creation

Label validation:
- Labels must start with a letter, contain [a-zA-Z0-9._:-], end alphanumeric
- 1-63 characters, case-sensitive, commas/spaces/equals banned
- Colon allowed for ad-hoc namespacing (e.g. project:myapp, env:dev)
- Validation on write paths only (run, label); filter paths accept any string

Safety improvements:
- All child PID operations verify create-time to prevent PID reuse
- Consolidated helper for verified child termination
- rm --all now gracefully stops running tasks (consistent with rm --labels)

Comprehensive help:
- topLevelHelp rewritten for AI agent comprehensiveness
- JSON output schemas, restart policy behavior, port detection documented
- State directory, label syntax/rules, variadic names, all short flags
- Expanded examples covering multi-name ops, labels, JSON piping

Bug fixes:
- --tail 0 shows zero lines (was showing all)
- dead status colored red (was yellow)
- Windows IsAlive fixed (WaitForSingleObject)
- Windows graceful stop via ctl file
- Graceful child termination (SIGTERM + timeout before SIGKILL)
- Tags renamed to Labels throughout (--labels/-l flag)
- Error messages use --labels (plural, matching flag name)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
dts: sun9i: increase BCM4330 post-power-on delay to 1000 ms

The BCM4330 WiFi chip on Cubieboard4 sometimes fails to respond to
SDIO commands after a cold power-on sequence.  Symptoms: repeated
'sunxi-mmc 1c10000.mmc: fatal err update clk timeout' messages every
~750 ms after mmc1 initializes, brcmfmac never loads, wlan0 absent.

The 500 ms post-power-on delay in mmc-pwrseq-simple was not always
sufficient to let the chip complete its internal power-on reset before
the SDIO host started clock-update commands.  Doubling it to 1000 ms
gives the BCM4330 more headroom to stabilize, especially on eMMC boots
where U-Boot no longer partially initializes the SDIO controller
(mmc1 is disabled in the U-Boot DTS via patch 0002).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
